speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Where is the fellow?

speaker: ALEXAS
text: Half afeard to come.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Go to, go to.—Come hither, sir.

speaker: ALEXAS
text: Good Majesty,

speaker: ALEXAS
text: Herod of Jewry dare not look upon you

speaker: ALEXAS
text: But when you are well pleased.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That Herod’s head

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I’ll have! But how, when Antony is gone,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Through whom I might command it?—Come thou near.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Most gracious Majesty!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Did’st thou behold Octavia?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Ay, dread queen.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Where?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Madam, in Rome.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: I looked her in the face and saw her led

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Between her brother and Mark Antony.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Is she as tall as me?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: She is not, madam.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Didst hear her speak? Is she shrill-tongued or low?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Madam, I heard her speak. She is low-voiced.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That’s not so good. He cannot like her long.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Like her? O Isis, ’tis impossible!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I think so, Charmian: dull of tongue, and dwarfish!—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: What majesty is in her gait? Remember,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: If e’er thou looked’st on majesty.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: She creeps.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Her motion and her station are as one.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: She shows a body rather than a life,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: A statue than a breather.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Is this certain?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Or I have no observance.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Three in Egypt

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Cannot make better note.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: He’s very knowing.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I do perceive ’t. There’s nothing in her yet.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The fellow has good judgment.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Excellent.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Guess at her years, I prithee.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Madam, she was a widow.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Widow? Charmian, hark.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And I do think she’s thirty.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Bear’st thou her face in mind? Is ’t long or round?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Round even to faultiness.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: For the most part, too, they are foolish that are so.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Her hair what color?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Brown, madam, and her forehead

speaker: MESSENGER
text: As low as she would wish it.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: There’s gold for thee.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Thou must not take my former sharpness ill.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I will employ thee back again. I find thee

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Most fit for business. Go, make thee ready.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Our letters are prepared.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: A proper man.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Indeed he is so. I repent me much

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That so I harried him. Why, methinks, by him,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: This creature’s no such thing.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Nothing, madam.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The man hath seen some majesty, and should know.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Hath he seen majesty? Isis else defend,

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: And serving you so long!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I have one thing more to ask him yet, good Charmian,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: But ’tis no matter. Thou shalt bring him to me

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Where I will write. All may be well enough.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: I warrant you, madam.
